name: Runtime NaN/Inf rejection at apply_output common write — Go/Java byte-exact
description: Locks the apply_output common-write NaN/Inf rejection message for Go and Java byte-exactly. Both runtimes let the Lua-emitted NaN propagate to ApplyOutput's validateValue. Python and C++ lua_bridge reject NaN earlier (lua-return-value layer) so their messages mention lua specifically — that is a legitimate implementation difference, surfaced before apply_output. R10-1 ensured Go and Java share the exact prefix shape (Go scheduler dropped the redundant `apply output:` wrap so it matches Java's direct ExecutionError path).

expected_error.wrapping_exact: pine: execution error in operator "produce_nan_common": common write: field "ratio": NaN/Inf is not a valid JSON value
